title: celmisia verbascifolia subsp. rigida
intro: Celmisia verbascifolia subsp. rigida, commonly known as the Mountain Celery or Celery-leaved Daisy, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Celmisia verbascifolia subsp. rigida can be used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
